title: Smartphone and Smartwatch-Based Biometrics Using Activities of Daily Living

biometric.body_location: right pants pocket and dominant hand/wrist

data.dataset_name: WISDM human activity recognition and biometrics data set

method.input_representation: Forty-three high-level features (mean, std dev, etc.) extracted from 10-second windows
method.training_strategy: Stratified 10-fold cross validation for identification; specific partitioning with 1:3 class ratio for authentication

evaluation.context: Achieved for Jogging activity using Accels (phone+watch accelerometers) with majority voting (50s data)

evaluation_protocol: Authentication evaluated via EER; Identification evaluated via accuracy (though accuracy is not extracted per rules). Decision making compared between single 10s example and majority voting over five 10s examples.

reported_limitations: Performance is quite sensitive to the amount of training data

reproducibility.benchmark_used: WISDM human activity recognition and biometrics data set

key_contribution: Comprehensive evaluation of 18 different activities of daily living for biometrics
Comparison between smartphone, smartwatch, and combined sensor configurations
Analysis of the impact of training data volume on EER and accuracy
main_strength: Systematic comparison of multiple ML algorithms and sensor combinations
main_weakness: Data collected in a controlled laboratory environment
Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack testing
insights.notes_for_review: The study demonstrates that accelerometers are significantly more effective than gyroscopes for behavioral biometrics and that combining sensors from both a phone and a watch provides the best performance.
